source: CIS AWS COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.1.0
title: Ensure only trusted images are used with Amazon ECS

input_row.source: CIS AWS COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.1.0
input_row.title: Ensure only trusted images are used with Amazon ECS
input_row.description: Ensure that only trusted container images from verified sources or private repositories are used with Amazon ECS to maintain the integrity and security of workloads. Note: This recommendation assumes that only the latest active revision of a task definition is in use. If older revisions are in use, apply the audit and remediation procedures to those revisions as needed.
input_row.rationale: Using trusted images reduces the risk of vulnerabilities, malware, or unauthorized modifications compromising ECS tasks. Impact: Minor costs for scanning, storage, and administrative effort to enforce policies and manage approved images.
input_row.audit: From Console: 1. Login to the ECS console using https://console.aws.amazon.com/ecs/. 2. In the left panel, click Task definitions. 3. Click the name of a task definition. 4. Click on the latest active revision of the task definition. 5. Click JSON. 6. For each element under containerDefinitions, ensure that image is set to an image trusted by your organization. 7. Repeat steps 1-6 for each task definition. From Command Line: Run the following command to list task definitions: aws ecs list-task-definitions For the latest revision of a task definition, run the following command: aws ecs describe-task-definition --task-definition <task-definition-arn> -- query 'taskDefinition.containerDefinitions[*].image' Ensure that the command returns only images trusted by your organization. Repeat for each task definition.
input_row.remediation: From Console: 1. Login to the ECS console using https://console.aws.amazon.com/ecs/. 2. In the left panel, click Task definitions. 3. Click the name of a task definition. 4. Click on the latest active revision of the task definition. 5. Click Create new revision. 6. Click Create new revision with JSON. 7. For each element under containerDefinitions, set image to an appropriate image trusted by your organization. 8. Repeat steps 1-7 for each task definition requiring remediation. Note: When a task definition is updated, running tasks launched from the previous task definition remain unchanged. Updating a running task requires redeploying it with the new task definition. References: 1. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/developerguide/container- considerations.html 2. https://awscli.amazonaws.com/v2/documentation/api/latest/reference/ecs/list- task-definitions.html 3. https://awscli.amazonaws.com/v2/documentation/api/latest/reference/ecs/describ e-task-definition.html
input_row.impact: Minor costs for scanning, storage, and administrative effort to enforce policies and manage approved images.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control can be fully verified using AWS APIs and CLI commands. The instructions provided in the audit section and the references include specific AWS CLI commands that can retrieve the necessary information about the images used in ECS task definitions. AWS APIs/boto3 can effectively enumerate task definitions and their container image details, making this control suitable for automated checks.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_ecs_task_definition_image_trusted_check

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **AWS API calls or boto3 SDK methods to use:**
  - Use `boto3.client('ecs').list_task_definitions()` to list all ECS task definitions.
  - Use `boto3.client('ecs').describe_task_definition(taskDefinition=task_definition_arn)` to retrieve the container definitions for each task definition.

- **AWS CLI commands:**
  - Use `aws ecs list-task-definitions` to get a list of all task definitions.
  - Use `aws ecs describe-task-definition --task-definition <task-definition-arn>` to fetch the container definitions, specifically examining the `image` field.

- **Specific resources to enumerate:**
  - Enumerate all task definitions within the AWS account.
  - Check all container definitions in the latest active revision of each task for trusted images.

- **Exact configuration properties/settings to validate:**
  - Verify that each image in the `containerDefinitions` under the `taskDefinition` is from a trusted source or private repository as per organizational policy.

- **Clear Pass/Fail criteria with expected values:**
  - **Pass:** All container images in ECS task definitions are from trusted registries or repositories.
  - **Fail:** One or more container images are from untrusted sources.

By implementing these automation details, the security posture regarding trusted images within ECS task definitions can be efficiently and effectively assessed across all applicable AWS regions.
